summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp at the package root that contains GYP command-expansion syntax (<!(...)) inside its sources/targets configuration. npm implicitly runs `node-gyp rebuild` whenever a binding.gyp is present — even without any declared install/postinstall script — and node-gyp evaluates <!(...) expressions as shell during the configure step. This causes the embedded command to execute automatically on `npm install`, functionally equivalent to a lifecycle hook. The package is published as a Backstage plugin (pure JavaScript/TypeScript surface) and does not require a native addon, so a binding.gyp performing command-expansion has no legitimate build purpose here. Stage analysis engaged on the file contents and its output was withheld by the provider safety filter, which is a corroborating signal that the traced content matched operational-malware patterns.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
